result.summary: Sendbird exposes five documented REST API surfaces for Chat, Calls, Live, Desk, and Business Messaging, each using dashboard-issued API tokens; no public GraphQL, MCP server, or CLI surfaced in public docs.
result.description: Sendbird provides APIs and SDKs for in-app chat, voice/video calls, live streaming, customer support, and business messaging. Its products let companies add real-time communication and support workflows to their applications.

app_api_token.label: Sendbird application API token (master or secondary)
app_api_token.setup: Sign in to the [Sendbird Dashboard](https://dashboard.sendbird.com), select your application, then go to **Settings → Application → General → API tokens**. Use the existing **master API token** or create a **secondary API token** from the Chat Platform API flow linked in [Generate a secondary API token](https://sendbird.com/docs/chat/platform-api/v3/application/managing-api-tokens/generate-a-secondary-api-token). The docs state the master token is created with the application and can't be revoked or changed; secondary tokens can be generated and revoked.
